rationale: Load-bearing files and symbols that anchor TokenKey's sixth platform `kiro` (AWS Kiro / CodeWhisperer). Kiro rides on a vendored protocol layer (internal/integration/kiro, sourced from Quorinex/Kiro-Go under MIT) exposed through the native Anthropic /v1/messages path, plus thin TK-only injection points. If any sentinel below is missing, the platform either silently disappears from scheduling/UI or fails at runtime. Single source of truth read by both `scripts/preflight.sh` (kiro sentinel registry section) and `.github/workflows/upstream-merge-pr-shape.yml` (on `merge/upstream-*` PRs). Adding a new load-bearing surface for kiro MUST add a sentinel here in the same commit.

sentinels.rationale: Identity constant for the sixth platform string `kiro`. Removing it collapses every kiro invariant (scheduling pool partition, Forward dispatch branch, account validation).

Kiro must be in AllSchedulingPlatforms() so background sweeps/snapshot hydration include the kiro-only pool; it must NOT be in OpenAICompatPlatforms() (kiro speaks CodeWhisperer, not OpenAI-compat). Losing the AllSchedulingPlatforms entry silently drops kiro accounts from rate-limit reaping and snapshot scheduling.
TK-side canonical KiroIDE client identity + User-Agent builder used for fingerprint mimicry. If dropped, kiro egress loses its KiroIDE-<ver> UA and is trivially distinguishable from the real client.
Provenance + re-vendor procedure for the MIT-licensed protocol layer. Deleting it severs the audit trail required to re-sync the vendored Kiro protocol when the upstream Kiro client changes.
Anthropic <-> Kiro request/response translation. Without it the /v1/messages -> CodeWhisperer bridge cannot build a payload or decode the reply.

Streaming call entry + hand-rolled AWS EventStream binary decoder + the HTTPDoer injection seam that lets the TK gateway supply a TLS/proxy-aware doer. Losing parseEventStream breaks all streaming responses; losing the HTTPDoer seam breaks fingerprint/proxy egress.

Account-domain predicate + the mapper that converts a TK account's credentials into the vendored kiroproto.Account. Losing IsKiro() removes kiro from the Forward dispatch branch and the scheduling pool; losing toKiroProtoAccount breaks every upstream call.

The sixth-platform forwarder: translates /v1/messages onto the Kiro EventStream upstream and enforces the second ToS/enable gate (IsKiroEnabled). Losing it makes every kiro request fail or, worse, bypass the enable gate.
Translates Kiro callbacks into the canonical Anthropic SSE event sequence. Losing it breaks streaming /v1/messages response shape for kiro accounts (clients hang or mis-parse).

The Forward() dispatch injection point (next to IsBedrock) + the GatewayService.kiroGateway field. An upstream merge that rewrites Forward() or NewGatewayService() must preserve this branch, or kiro accounts silently fall through to the Anthropic path and fail.

First of the two ToS gates: account creation is rejected unless credentials.tos_acknowledged is true. Losing this lets kiro accounts be created without explicit ToS acknowledgement (compliance regression).
The default-off kiro enable flag read by the Forward gate. Losing it would either hard-disable kiro or (if defaulted wrong) enable outbound forwarding without the operator opt-in.
Implements OAuthRefreshExecutor for kiro (1h SSO tokens, social + IdC endpoints). Losing it stops kiro token auto-refresh; accounts silently expire and drop out of the pool with no recovery.

Registration of KiroTokenRefresher into the background refresh ticker's refreshers[]/executors[]. An upstream merge that rewrites NewTokenRefreshService must preserve this line, or kiro refresh never runs even though the refresher type still exists.

The RPM-increment gate (both the streaming and non-streaming paths) admits kiro accounts via `|| account.IsKiro()`. An upstream merge that rewrites these gates must preserve the kiro arm, or kiro accounts skip RPM accounting and bypass the per-account rate limit.

Wire provider registration for the kiro forwarder (NewKiroGatewayService). Dropping it de-wires the gateway's kiro branch dependency. (The KiroTokenRefresher is constructed inside NewTokenRefreshService, not via wire — its registration is guarded by the token_refresh_service.go sentinel entry instead.)
Load-bearing QA evidence for the account-creation ToS gate (matches the *_tk_*.go hotspot rule). Losing it removes the regression guard around the first ToS acknowledgement check.
Kiro's membership in GATEWAY_PLATFORMS. Removing it drops kiro from every admin platform picker/badge (the same silent-UI-regression mode the newapi frontend sentinels guard).
Kiro's brand label entry. Without it the kiro platform renders an untranslated key or is dropped from option lists.
The kiro branch + field component wiring in the account-create modal. Losing it makes operators unable to create kiro accounts from the admin UI.
English i18n strings for the kiro account form (labels, ToS copy, validation messages). Losing them surfaces raw i18n keys in the UI.
Chinese i18n strings for the kiro account form. Kept paired with en.ts so the two locales never drift for kiro.
